mission.title: containerd: containerd-shim processes leak during high pod churn
mission.description: containerd-shim processes leak during high pod churn. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
## Problem Description

containerd-shim-runc-v2 processes are leaking under high pod churn scenarios in containerd
v2.x, causing gradual memory growth and process accumulation on nodes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for containerd-shim processes leak during high pod churn
steps.description:
Based on current information, I think it's known issue. If we can allow to delete shim multiple times after failure, it should be fixed easily. Any retry here is just workaround. More information is here - https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/8981
```yaml
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

helm install monitoring prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \
  --create-namespace --namespace monitoring \
  --set grafana.adminPassword=xxxx

# setup port forwarding
# this makes the dashboard available at http://localhost:3000
kubectl port-forward -n monitoring svc/monitoring-grafana 3000:80
```

steps.title: Confirm containerd-shim processes leak during high pod… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Based on current information, I think it's known issue. If we can allow to delete shim multiple times after failure, it should be fixed easily. Any retry here is just workaround. More information is here - https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/8981
codeSnippets:
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

helm install monitoring prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \
  --create-namespace --namespace monitoring \
  --set grafana.adminPassword=xxxx

# setup port forwarding
# this makes the dashboard available at http://localhost:3000
kubectl port-forward -n monitoring svc/monitoring-grafana 3000:80

# Create 200 CronJobs with direct kubectl apply
for i in $(seq 1 200); do
  kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: test-cronjob-$i
  namespace: cronjob-test
spec:
  schedule: "* * * * *"
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  concurrencyPolicy: Replace
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 30
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: test-job
            image: busybox:latest
            imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/shm/test bs=1M count=30
              sleep 3
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "50Mi"
                cpu: "10m"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
